prose: This positive call rests on curated CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence, a diagnostic anchor that carries the reasoning on its own [E1]. Mechanistically, the evidence-ranked hypothesis is initiation at Complex-I Q-site subunits, grounded in MitoCarta3.0 annotation, with the adverse outcome converging on the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population—linking a plausible molecular initiating event to the cell type selectively lost in parkinsonism [E2]. Brain-penetrance modeling supports plausible CNS exposure, making that target engagement spatially coherent [E5], while the classic Langston human case series describes causal parkinsonism consistent with this axis [E4]. Assay data are thin in ToxCast, reflecting MPTP's status as a research reagent rather than a routinely screened chemical; this corroboration is anti-diagnostic and should not be read as decisive either way [E3]. The tier remains curated_anchored_only: confidence derives from the curated mechanism, not from bioactivity breadth, and no strand here weakens that anchor.

citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP-3 (WPHA/WNT Endorsed): inhibition of mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (Terron A, Bal-Price A, et al. Arch Toxicol. 2018;92:41-82). AOP-3 anchors a convergent mitochondrial-complex family — complex II/III/IV and redox cycling (AOPs 588/587/589/593, under development) — whose formal expansion EFSA funded in 2024 (call NP/EFSA/PREV/2024/02). For Alzheimer's: the endorsed AOP-12/48 (aging neurodegeneration + memory) anchor with a non-endorsed Tau/amyloid overlay (AOP-429/475).

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
citations.reference: Rath S, Sharma R, Gupta R, et al. MitoCarta3.0: an updated mitochondrial proteome now with sub-organelle localization and pathway annotations. Nucleic Acids Research. 2021;49(D1):D1541-D1547. Kamath T, Abdulraouf A, Burris SJ, et al. Single-cell genomic profiling of human dopamine neurons identifies a population that selectively degenerates in Parkinson's disease. Nature Neuroscience. 2022;25(5):588-595.
U.S. EPA ToxCast/Tox21 invitroDB (v4.2), accessed through the NICEATM Integrated Chemical Environment (ICE). Active hitcalls only; queried on the salt-form-correct record.

causal parkinsonism (case series) (Langston 1983 (Science)). Quantified PD/AD exposure risk curated from published cohorts and meta-analyses (e.g., Tanner et al. 2011, Environmental Health Perspectives; Pezzoli & Cereda 2013, Neurology).

Meng F, Xi Y, Huang J, Ayers PW. A curated diverse molecular database of blood-brain barrier permeability with chemical descriptors (B3DB). Scientific Data. 2021;8:289. Daina A, Zoete V. A BOILED-Egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain access of small molecules. ChemMedChem. 2016;11(11):1117-1121.
